name: Leukemia
description: Leukemia — malignancy of blood-forming cells; one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.

en.name: Leukemia
en.shortDescription: Leukemia — malignancy of blood-forming cells; one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Leukemia is a malignancy of the blood-forming (haematopoietic) cells, producing abnormal white cells in the blood and bone marrow. In intensive care it is a major comorbidity (cytopenias, immunosuppression, complications of chemotherapy) and a cause of admission. Leukemia is one of the chronic health items in the APACHE IV severity model.

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *317510 / 93143009 — Leukemia* with all descendants included, capturing acute and chronic leukemias, lymphoid and myeloid lineages, and their subtypes (including leukemia in remission).

## Mapping Notes

- Lymphoma and multiple myeloma are related haematologic malignancies captured by their own sets.
- Leukemia in remission is included in this tree; the set records the diagnosis, not the disease activity status.
